label: External Market User (Non-Customer Segment)

segment.role: Mid-career professional evaluating tools to solve operational inefficiencies and career advancement challenges
segment.context: They are cautiously exploring solutions outside their current vendor ecosystem due to mounting frustrations with poor support and operational friction. Their evaluation process is heavily influenced by concerns about AI disruption to their role and the need to demonstrate measurable business impact.

goals.goal: Find automated analysis tools that reduce manual work without sacrificing accuracy
Evaluate AI-powered solutions while maintaining skepticism about data quality
Identify methodologically rigorous research frameworks for immediate implementation
Assess tools that help demonstrate ROI and business impact to leadership
Explore solutions for seamless team handoffs and data consolidation

constraints: Limited budget approval authority requiring strong ROI justification (from Corporate Advancement Barrier)
Must work within existing tech stack due to organizational friction (from Operational Friction pain points)
Cannot afford lengthy implementation cycles due to competitive job market pressure (from AI Role Disruption Anxiety)
Requires scientific rigor and clear methodologies to maintain credibility (from Methodological Rigor Concern)
behavioral_rules: Immediately searches for pricing information upfront to avoid wasting time on unaffordable solutions
Scans support documentation quality before engaging with sales to assess vendor expertise level
Looks for specific methodology details and scientific backing rather than marketing claims
Checks for integration capabilities with existing tools to avoid operational disruption
Seeks out case studies showing measurable business impact and ROI metrics
Tests free trials or demos extensively before committing to any paid engagement
Reads reviews specifically looking for mentions of support quality and account management
Abandons vendors who require lengthy sales processes without transparent pricing
Prioritizes vendors with clear implementation timelines and minimal setup friction
Verifies AI accuracy claims through hands-on testing rather than trusting marketing materials
deal_breakers: Requires external vendors for basic implementation tasks
Lacks responsive support forums or documentation
Uses predatory auto-renewal contracts with poor cancellation processes
Cannot demonstrate clear ROI metrics or business impact
AI-generated insights show obvious hallucinations or quality issues
voice_sample: I'm honestly exhausted from dealing with vendors who promise the world but can't even provide basic support when things go wrong. The last platform we tried had such chaotic account management - our contact changed three times in six months and each one knew less than the previous. I need something that actually works without requiring me to become a technical expert overnight. The pressure to adopt AI tools is real, but I'm not falling for solutions that generate pretty reports full of hallucinated data. I need automated thematic analysis that saves time while maintaining accuracy, and I need to be able to show leadership exactly how it impacts our bottom line. Clear, actionable frameworks that can be implemented immediately - that's what I'm looking for, not another tool that creates more problems than it solves.

browsing_patterns.tab_behavior: Opens multiple vendor comparison tabs, keeps pricing pages open for reference
browsing_patterns.primary_device: Work laptop during business hours for evaluation, mobile for quick research
browsing_patterns.time_of_day: Mid-morning and late afternoon when not in meetings

purchase_journey.discovery: Finds solutions through professional networks complaining about current vendor issues and industry forum recommendations
purchase_journey.evaluation: Starts with pricing transparency, moves to methodology documentation, then support quality assessment, finally hands-on testing
purchase_journey.decision_moment: When they can clearly articulate ROI to leadership and verify the solution reduces manual work without quality loss
purchase_journey.post_purchase_expectation: Seamless onboarding with expert support, immediate time savings, and measurable business impact within 30 days

converts_when: Pricing is transparent and fits budget constraints
Support team demonstrates deep expertise in initial interactions
Free trial shows measurable time savings on actual work
abandons_when: Support staff lack expertise and require external vendors
Account management shows signs of chaos or frequent turnover
AI tools produce obviously inaccurate or biased results
Implementation requires significant technical skills they don't have
trusts_when: Vendor provides comprehensive code samples and responsive author support
Clear frameworks can be implemented immediately
Business impact metrics are measurable and verifiable
becomes_suspicious_when: Pricing involves nickel-and-diming or hidden fees
Product information seems outdated or disconnected from current practices
AI claims seem too good to be true without validation
Sales process feels predatory or high-pressure

phrases_they_use: Manual thematic analysis and coding of open-ended survey responses is extremely time-consuming for analysts
Chaotic account management with frequent staff turnover
Support staff lack deep expertise, requiring external vendors for basic tasks
Clear, actionable frameworks that can be implemented immediately
Ability to clearly demonstrate the ROI of UXR work to secure budget and headcount
AI-generated summaries from call transcripts are unreliable or hallucinate details
Confusion between qualitative and quantitative research, leading to poor study design
resonates_with: Automated analysis that maintains scientific rigor
Proven ROI metrics and business impact
Expert support with deep domain knowledge
turned_off_by: Vague promises without concrete implementation details
Account management instability and support gaps

inner_monologue_samples: Here we go again with hidden pricing - if I have to 'contact sales' just to see if this fits our budget, they're probably targeting enterprise customers with enterprise budgets.
These case studies look impressive but I need to see the actual methodology behind these ROI claims - too many vendors just throw around numbers without showing their work.
The navigation on this site is confusing and if they can't organize their own website properly, what does that say about their internal processes and support quality?
Another signup form asking for my phone number and company size before I can even see a demo - this feels like they're more interested in their sales pipeline than helping me evaluate the solution.
Finally, a vendor that shows actual code samples and has responsive documentation - this gives me confidence they understand the technical challenges I'm facing daily.
anti_patterns: Assuming they want cutting-edge AI features over proven reliability (contradicts AI Research Skepticism cluster)
Pushing complex technical solutions when they need simple automation (contradicts Analysis Automation Seekers needs)
Focusing on enterprise features when they need practical ROI justification (contradicts Corporate Advancement Barrier challenges)
Minimizing support quality concerns when operational friction is their top pain point (contradicts Operational Friction cluster)
Treating them as early adopters when they exhibit strong risk aversion and methodological concerns (contradicts low risk tolerance from data)

traits.label: Operational Friction & Poor Support
traits.description: Users experience significant operational issues stemming from disorganized internal processes, insufficient expertise, and poor communication across teams. This creates a frustrating experience where basic tasks become difficult and trust in the product/service erodes.
key_phrases: Chaotic account management with frequent staff turnover
Support staff lack deep expertise, requiring external vendors for basic tasks
Disconnect and poor coordination between different internal teams
Frustration with missing sample code and unresponsive support forums
Consistently poor and unknowledgeable customer support

traits.label: AI Role Disruption Anxiety
traits.description: Product managers are grappling with anxiety about how artificial intelligence is reshaping their role, creating tension between wanting to maintain strategic focus and feeling pressure to adopt technical skills to stay relevant.
key_phrases: Pressure to adopt AI in ways that conflict with role identity or core responsibilities
Concern about job disruption from AI enabling others to build products with minimal technical expertise
Feeling that actual work of product management is shifting to something undesirable
Amplify core PM work (discovery, planning, strategy) with AI to free up time for higher-value activities
Stay relevant in job market by developing technical skills to complement strategic skills

traits.label: Corporate Advancement Barrier
traits.description: Frustration with navigating organizational politics, gaining visibility, and demonstrating financial impact to advance careers in competitive corporate environments. Professionals seek practical strategies to overcome dysfunction, speak the language of executives, and secure promotions despite systemic challenges.
key_phrases: navigating a highly competitive job market
difficulty getting visibility and demonstrating impact within large organizations
difficulty tying product impact to financial metrics (P&L) to gain influence

traits.label: AI Research Skepticism
traits.description: Users are cautiously optimistic about AI-powered research tools for efficiency but express deep concerns about the accuracy, bias, and strategic value of AI-generated insights, fearing they may replace genuine human feedback with misleading data.
key_phrases: Concerns about AI-generated data lacking genuine variation and being inherently biased.
Suspicion that providers artificially shrink variance in synthetic data to increase confidence unnaturally.
Anxiety about AI's inability to deliver strategic insights due to hallucinations and quality issues.

traits.label: Analysis Automation Seekers
traits.description: These users are researchers or analysts who need tools to automate the tedious, manual work of analyzing qualitative data and improving survey efficiency, while maintaining accuracy and depth.
key_phrases: Manual thematic analysis and coding of open-ended survey responses is extremely time-consuming for analysts.
Time-consuming manual analysis of large volumes of unstructured qualitative data (e.g., 'a thousand comments - how do you make it useful?').
Automated thematic analysis from interviews and open-ended responses that is deep, accurate, and reduces manual work.

traits.label: Inefficient Handoff Pain
traits.description: Users are frustrated by the manual effort and information loss during the handoff between sales and customer success teams. They seek automated, accurate tools that unify customer data across sources to enable seamless transitions.
key_phrases: Manual aggregation of customer data from multiple sources (calls, emails, Slack, notes) is time-consuming and tedious
Sales-to-CS handoff lacks critical context; CSMs are often unprepared, leading to customer frustration and churn risk
Walking into customer calls without proper context or documentation of promises made is a high-stress 'resume-generating event'

traits.label: Methodological Rigor Concern
traits.description: Users are frustrated by research content that lacks scientific rigor, practical guidance, or ethical standards. They seek clear, actionable, and well-founded methodologies that can be reliably applied to real-world business problems.
key_phrases: confusion between qualitative and quantitative research, leading to poor study design
frustration with unclear or incomplete information in research materials
clear, actionable frameworks that can be implemented immediately

traits.label: Inclusive Research Friction
traits.description: Frustrated advocacy for integrating inclusive, accessibility-focused user research into organizational workflows, often encountering resistance due to perceived high costs and unclear business value.
key_phrases: Difficulty accessing or recruiting participants from non-customer segments (e.g., people with disabilities) for research
Internal resistance and lack of leadership support for accessibility or inclusive research, leading to conflict and burnout
Clear, measurable evidence linking accessibility/inclusive research to business outcomes (e.g., ROI, reduced risk)

traits.label: AI Support Implementation Doubt
traits.description: Users express concerns about AI-powered support tools failing to meet expectations due to poor handling of edge cases, high maintenance overhead, and inability to effectively triage complex issues. They desire automation that frees human agents for nuanced work but fear solutions that create more problems than they solve.
key_phrases: AI chatbots fail at edge cases, emotional queries, or complex troubleshooting
High cost of advanced AI agents that handle edge cases and triage effectively
Seamless handoff to human support for edge cases, emotional issues, or complex problems

traits.label: Evaluative Consideration
traits.description: This group represents users who are in a deliberate consideration phase, showing neither strong aversion to friction nor immediate purchase intent. They appear to be weighing their options carefully, balancing the effort required with the perceived value of the product.

issues: Very few negative reviews — pain points may be underrepresented
Platform bias: amazon is 50% of data (cap is 40%) — signals may reflect platform culture, not demographic behavior. Add other sources.

demographic_description: A user outside the existing customer base whose needs are considered for expansion. Interaction involves understanding broader market requirements beyond current users. Feedback is gathered through outreach beyond existing accounts.
